fields.helpText: This field identifies the patient by establishing a pointer to either the VA Patient File, or, for non-VA patients, to the Referral File.  Enter the patient's name to see if it is already on file.  If not, you will need to enter the name, along with other identifiers, into the appropriate file. Contact your MAS representative if you have difficulty entering a new patient into the VA Patient File.
Identifies the last name of the patient.  For further information see FORDS page 39.
RECORD THE PATIENT'S FIRST AND LAST NAME.
RECORD PATIENT'S LAST NAME IF IT CONTAINS A C'.
Looks at sex field, and determines how letter should be addressed.
Identifies the middle name or middle initial of the patient.  For further information see FORDS page 41.
RECORD FIRST LINE OF PATIENT'S ADDRESS.
RECORD 2ND LINE OF PATIENT'S ADDRESS.
RECORD 3RD LINE OF PATIENT'S ADDRESS.
RECORD THE PATIENT'S STATE OF RESIDENCE.
RECORD THE PATIENT'S RESIDENCE ZIP CODE.
Computes the patient's current COUNTY of residence. COUNTY is derived from COUNTY (2,.117).
THIS IS A COMPUTED FIELD THAT BRINGS UPS THE COUNTY OF RESIDENCE COMPUTED FROM ZIP CODE.
Identifies the patient's current address (number and street).  For further information see FORDS page 49.
Provides the ability to store additional address information such as the name of a place or facility (ie, a nursing home or name of an apartment complex).  For further information see FORDS page 50.
This is the patient's county, retrieved from the PATIENT file (#2).  If the ONCOLOGY PATIENT file is pointing to the REFERRAL file (#67), the CTY value will be null.
Records the current telphone number with area code for the patient.  For further information see FORDS page 55.
THIS IS A COMPUTED FIELD FOR THE PATIENT'S CONTACTS.
THIS FIELD CONTAINS INFORMATION FOR THE PATIENT'S NEXT OF KIN.
THIS FIELD CONTAINS INFORMATION RELATED TO THE PATIENT'S 2ND NEXT OF KIN.
THIS FIELD CONTAINS INFORMATION REGARDING THE NEXT OF KIN RELATIONSHIP TO THE PATIENT.
THIS FIELD CONTAINS INFORMATION REGARDING THE 2ND NEXT OF KIN RELATIONSHIP TO THE PATIENT.
This is the patient's next of kin, retrieved from either the PATIENT File (#2) or the REFERRAL File (#67).
COMPUTED FIELD BRINGING OVER ANY ALIAS LISTED IN THE MAS FILE FOR THE PATIENT.
Records the patient's Social Security number.  For further information see FORDS page 37.
COMPUTED SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBER.
Identifies the date of birth of the patient.  For further information see FORDS page 57.
Identifies the date of birth of the patient in internal FileMan format.
Records the medical record number usually assigned by the reporting facility's health information management (HIM) department.  For further information see FORDS page 36.
COMPUTED FIELD TAKING THE LAST TWO DIGITS FROM THE PATIENT'S SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBER.
Regional data set - One number per patient - Can be left blank.

The case number is issued by the SEER participant to identify the person. Each computer record pertaining to the same person must have an identical case number.  It uses eight digits with leading zeros as necessary.
Records the patient's place of birth.  For further information see FORDS page 56.
Identifies the primary race of the person.  For further information see FORDS page 59.
Identifies the patient's race.  For further information see FORDS page 61.
Identifies the patient's race.  For further information see FORDS page 62.
Identifies the patient's race.  For further information see FORDS page 63.
Identifies the patient's race.  For further information see FORDS page 64.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:Non-Spanish, non-Hispanic;1:Mexican;2:Puerto Rican;3:Cuban;4:South/Central American;5:Other specified Spanish/Hispanic;6:Spanish, Hispanic, Latino, NOS;7:Spanish surname only;8:Dominican Republic;9:Unknown/not stated;
fields.helpText: Identifies persons of Spanish or Hispanic origin.  NOTE: Code 8 (Dominican Republic) is for use with patients who       were diagnosed with cancer on January 1, 2005, or later.  For further information see FORDS page 65.

fields.setOfCodes: 1:Male;2:Female;3:Other (intersex, disorders of sexual development/DSD);4:Transsexual, NOS;5:Transsexual, natal male;6:Transsexual, natal female;9:Not stated;
fields.helpText: Identifies the sex of the patient.  For further information see FORDS page 66.
COMPUTED FIELD WITH THE LABORATORY DATA FILE NUMBER.
This is the current occupation of the patient.
THE PATIENT'S RELIGION.
COMPUTED FIELD LISTING THE PATIENT'S MARTIAL STATUS.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:Dead;1:Alive;

RECORDS THE PATIENT'S LAST FOLLOW-UP CONTACT.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:Inactive;1:Active;8:LTF;
fields.helpText: RECORDS THE PATIENT'S FOLLOW UP STATUS.

This field will display a division specific count of a patient's primaries.

fields.setOfCodes: D:Directly related;I:Indirectly related;N:Not related;U:Unknown;
fields.helpText: This field contains a code indicating whether the patient died as a result of cancer.

Record the CAUSE OF DEATH listed on the death certificate.  Central registries are the primary users of this data item.  Use the underlying CAUSE OF DEATH (ICD code) identified by state health department.

fields.setOfCodes: 7777:State death certificate or listing not available;7797:State death cert available, cause of death not coded;
fields.helpText: This field stores special CAUSE OF DEATH values which are not ICD codes.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:Patient alive at last follow-up;8:ICDA-8;9:ICD-9;
fields.helpText: This information will be provided by the central registry when backloading cases.  It refers to which ICD coding scheme (see Field 19 'Cause of Death') used to code the cause of death.
Enter the place of death.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:Patient alive;1:Autopsy performed;2:No autopsy performed;9:Patient expired, unknown if autopsy performed;
fields.helpText: This field contains a code indicating (if known) whether a post-mortem examination was performed on the patient.

RECORD THE AUTOPSY SEQUENCE NUMBER, USUALLY STATED AS  A-90-XXXX.

Computes the SERVICE BRANCH [LAST] (#2,.325) value for this patient.

This field will compute a value of either 0 or 1.   0 = All ONCOLOGY PRIMARY (#165.5) records for this patient have a CLASS      CATEGORY (#165.5,.042) value of 0 (NONANALYTIC).   1 = At least one ONCOLOGY PRIMARY (#165.5) record for this patient has a      CLASS CATEGORY (#165.5,.042) value of 1 (ANALYTIC).   The default value is 0.

This field will compute a value of either 0 or 1.   0 = All ONCOLOGY PRIMARY (#165.5) records for this patient have a      CLASS OF CASE (#165.5,.04) value of "00" or greater than "22",      meaning either Analytic ("30" and above) or NOT Requiring       Follow-up ("00").   1 = At least one ONCOLOGY PRIMARY (#165.5) record for this patient has      a CLASS OF CASE (#165.5,.04) value in the range "10" thru "22",       meaning Analytic cases Requiring Follow-Up.   The default value is 0.
This field allows for the display of all tumor statuses for expired patients having multiple primaries.
The last name (surname) of patients at birth, regardless of gender or marital status, this data item is introduced in 2021 as a gender-neutral replacement for the NAACCR data item Name-Maiden [2390].

This multiple field stores the SUSPENSE data for each division where the patient is on SUSPENSE.

RECORDS SOURCE DOCUMENT.

FOLLOW-UP of cancer patients provides the following data needed for survival analysis:  the vital status of the patient, the date the vital status was determined, and the underlying cause of death, if the person has died.  SEER requires that this information be updated annually for living patients.
RECORDS THE DATE AND TIME A FOLLOW UP ATTEMPT IS MADE.
RECORDS THE CONTACT TYPE FOR A FOLLOW UP ATTEMPT.
